mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS declines remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power in late-cycle samples.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in retail sales and consumer prices indicate demand-pull inflation that forces monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive trend in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a combined regression coefficient insignificant over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid expansion in the industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth rates show no correlation with 5-day forward returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when tested on out-of-sample recovery data.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.5 million units signal robust construction activity and consumer confidence, which amplifies economic momentum and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly positive when HOUST surpasses 1500.0, or the threshold signal generates a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market that sustains household income and consumption, providing a consistent upward bias for S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes exhibit a Rank IC below 0.02 for 60-day forward returns across all tested regimes, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price index levels combined with rising federal funds rates confirm an active monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates and suppresses equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction between elevated CPIAUCSL and rising FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with the joint effect showing a positive or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth reflects broad-based economic expansion that boosts corporate profits and investor risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 60-day forward returns during early-cycle expansion phases, or the signal produces a negative cumulative return when applied to early-cycle historical data.
